steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between two independent clauses joined by the conjunctive adverb however: He did not play drums on the label's classic song "Rapper's [blank] and he joined the label after the song's release. The correct punctuation must separate two independent clauses and handle the quotation mark and however correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Delight." However,: the period splits the material into two separate sentences, but this placement makes however introduce the second sentence, incorrectly contrasting the after-release explanation with the previous sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — Delight," however;: the comma inside the closing quotation mark follows American punctuation convention; however serves as a parenthetical conjunctive adverb, and the semicolon after it properly separates the two independent clauses — the correct pattern is [IC,] conjunctive adverb; [IC]. ✓
  • Choice C — Delight," however,: uses a comma both before and after however, creating a comma splice — two independent clauses joined by only commas. ✗
  • Choice D — Delight" however: no punctuation around however at all. The two independent clauses run together with no separator. ✗
That matches Choice B — Delight," however;.
